headline: Amcor (AMCR) Stock Looks Cheap On Cash Flow But Rich On Earnings
summary: Amcor has gained 11.7% over the past month, yet there is a clear tension in how the stock is priced, with a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) intrinsic value estimate pointing to meaningful upside while earnings based multiples suggest the shares are on the expensive side. Recent portfolio moves, such as the sale of ESE World and new partnerships in sustainable and industrial packaging, add fresh context to that split in valuation signals. The 11.7% return over the past month leaves Amcor trading...
